title: `pypodman images` doesn't truncate a long image name like `podman images` does.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:
# **kind bug**

**Description**
Only happens when the image name is pretty long.
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. on remote:
```
# podman images
REPOSITORY                                                                 TAG       IMAGE ID       CREATED        SIZE
localhost/minimal-httpd                                                    latest    cc77fde4759b   23 hours ago   148MB
localhost/init-httpd                                                       latest    b540cee155c4   25 hours ago   222MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8-minimal         8.0-73    2d7409972c5e   10 days ago    89.4MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/net-snmp        5.8.2-1   bd8911a9446f   3 weeks ago    242MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/support-tools   8.0-1     1e342da039f6   3 weeks ago    178MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/rsyslog         8.0-1     1e97bbd0fe95   3 weeks ago    220MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/sadc            8.0-1     f40386559857   3 weeks ago    217MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/rhel-tools      8.0-2     be90b6f7718b   4 weeks ago    449MB
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhel8/rhel-init       8.0.0-1   5819904b7fbd   4 weeks ago    202MB
```


**Describe the results you received:**
on host:
```
# pypodman -l root --host 192.168.122.213 images                                
root@192.168.122.213's password:
REPOSITORY                               TAG        IMAGE ID     CREATED      SIZE                       
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 5819904b7fbd Sep 12       192.4 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 be90b6f7718b Sep 17       428.1 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 f40386559857 Sep 19       207.0 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 1e97bbd0fe95 Sep 19       209.4 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 1e342da039f6 Sep 20       169.3 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 bd8911a9446f Sep 21       230.5 Mi                   
brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.red 8888/rhel8 2d7409972c5e Oct 05       85.3 MiB                   
localhost/init-httpd                     latest     b540cee155c4 yesterday    211.8 Mi                   
localhost/minimal-httpd                  latest     cc77fde4759b yesterday    141.1 Mi
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
same output as the remote `podman images` shows.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
always

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @Edward5hen thanks for the report, I'm going to adjust the title a little bit.
